name: xray
voice: an imaging scanner reads the speech. A hot vertical scan beam sweeps a dark radiograph band and WIPES each body word in exactly as it crosses — instant just-traced flash, ice-blue phosphor bones. At the apex the beam freezes and LOCKS the hero word behind the subject: a bracket target-frame snaps shut, an x-ray exposure pulse fires, the word flips to a glowing negative, a loupe ring passes over each letter, and a MATCH readout types. Then the beam returns flipped and scans the freeze-frame away R→L while the later lines reprint in its wake.
when: imaging / sensing / AI / vision / measurement / forensics content, anything about detection, recovery, resolution, or seeing the unseen. Sleek-clinical register. Needs a darker mid-band tolerance for the radiograph band; the apex dim + exposure flash make their own contrast on bright skies.
